My notes: Nestled in a tranquil pocket of Torrance, CA, this brewery strikes the right balance between neighborhood gem and serious craft beer destination. The outdoor seating lends a casual, welcoming atmosphere, while street parking in the area is plentiful and hassle-free. A rotating food truck adds a fun, spontaneous element to the experience. The beer selection is well-curated with enough variety to satisfy most palates, and the tasting flight is an excellent way to explore the lineup. A must-try: the Squirrel Red Ale, which easily earns its spot as a house highlight. Plan your trip at https://www.smogcitybrewing.com/

Situated in a livelier stretch of Torrance, this brewery brings an energetic buzz that sets it apart from quieter spots in the area. Parking can require a bit of patience given the busier surroundings, but the experience inside is well worth the extra few minutes. An on-site food truck keeps the hunger at bay, while the outdoor seating area provides a great perch to soak in the atmosphere. The place earns its crowds. Expect a packed house on evenings and weekends, a testament to its local following. If you order nothing else, make it the Juicy IPA — it's the undisputed star of the tap list and reason enough to come back. Plan your visit at https://www.monkishbrewing.com/

Perfectly positioned in the heart of Mammoth Village, this brewery checks every box for an aprčs-adventure stop. Parking is refreshingly ample, a welcome relief in a mountain town, and the two-level layout offers plenty of seating to match any mood, whether you prefer the energy of the main floor or a quieter perch upstairs. The outdoor eating area is a natural extension of the mountain surroundings, and a thoughtful touch sets this place apart: a water barrel station for refilling canteens or hydrating your four-legged trail companions. The tasting experience is well structured, with two distinct flights of seven pours each, an ideal way to work through the lineup. Standouts include the Hop Spill Hazy, bright and approachable, and the Lair of the Bear Stout, rich and satisfying. On the food side, the falafel salad is a genuine highlight and holds its own against any brewery kitchen fare. And if you can snag a window seat, the reward is sweeping views of the Eastern Sierra, quite possibly the best backdrop of any brewery in the region. Plan your visit at https://mammothbrewingco.com/

A worthy detour on the drive between Los Angeles and San Diego, this brewery makes a compelling case for pulling off the freeway. The industrial-chic setting strikes an appealing balance between raw character and polished comfort, anchored by a solid restaurant and a spacious outdoor patio. Parking is plentiful, making the stop entirely stress-free. Beyond the beer, the brewery doubles as a destination in its own right. Merchandise including branded tees makes for a fitting souvenir to remember the visit. On the beer front, the Phantom Bride IPA is the clear standout, a well-crafted pour that alone justifies the stop. Whether you are breaking up a long drive or making a deliberate outing, this is one detour you will not regret. Plan your visit at https://pub980.belchingbeaver.com/vista-belching-beaver-pub-980-beer

If you hike mount tammany at the Delaware water gap, you should visit this brewery which is 15 mins off Rt.80 in Blairstown. Opened in 2015 they brew on-site and have a two level restaurant with ample parking. They have a good bar as well. Their Ahi tacos are yummy. Plan your visit at https://buckhillbrewery.com/
